title: Somatic Hearing
definition: The brain's ability to perceive rhythm, vibration, and pattern as a meaningful category of experience through the entire somatic self, without relying on auditory channels.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Even when someone is deaf, they can still 'hear' through other parts of their body like feeling vibrations or seeing rhythmic movements.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The brain repurposes and amplifies data from various sensory sources (touch, vision, proprioception) to create a coherent model of sound and music when auditory channels are absent or non-functional.
difficulty_levels.expert: The brain's innate ability to adapt and function in the face of sensory loss leads to an expansion of 'hearing' beyond the ear, creating a whole-body, synesthetic experience of rhythm and vibration.

extra_fields.insight: The human brain is a meaning-making machine that doesn't experience absence; it experiences what is present.
